name: Technical Developers
description: Software engineers, architects, and technical leads who value precision, examples, and practical implementation detail.

characteristics.knowledge_level: High technical literacy — comfortable with code, architecture patterns, and systems thinking
characteristics.expectations: Concrete examples, code snippets, specific tool recommendations, honest trade-off analysis
characteristics.priorities: Does it work? Is it correct? What are the edge cases?

communication.jargon_tolerance: 9/10 — domain terms expected, no need to define basics
communication.detail_level: High — show the implementation, not just the concept
communication.evidence_preference: Code examples, benchmarks, architecture diagrams, real-world incidents

resonates: Specific technical depth, honest about limitations, 'here is what actually happened in production', code you can run
alienates: Marketing speak, vague claims without evidence, oversimplification of complex topics, 'just use best practices'

name: Business Stakeholders
description: Executives, managers, and decision-makers who value outcomes, ROI, and strategic implications.

characteristics.knowledge_level: Business-literate, variable technical depth — assume competence but not domain expertise
characteristics.expectations: Clear recommendations, quantified impact, risk assessment, timeline and resource implications
characteristics.priorities: What does this mean for the business? What's the cost of action vs inaction?

communication.formality: 6/10 — professional but not stiff, confident tone
communication.jargon_tolerance: 3/10 — translate technical concepts, use business language
communication.detail_level: Executive summary first, detail available on request
communication.evidence_preference: Metrics, case studies, competitive analysis, financial projections

resonates: Clear bottom-line impact, actionable recommendations, competitive context, 'here is what I recommend and why'
alienates: Technical jargon without context, excessive detail before the conclusion, hedging without commitment

name: General Audience
description: Non-specialist readers who value clarity, relatability, and practical takeaways they can use immediately.

characteristics.knowledge_level: No assumed domain expertise — explain concepts from first principles
characteristics.expectations: Clear explanations, relatable examples, step-by-step guidance, visual aids
characteristics.priorities: Can I understand this? Can I do something with this? Is this relevant to me?

communication.jargon_tolerance: 1/10 — define every technical term, use analogies
communication.detail_level: Moderate — enough to understand, not enough to overwhelm
communication.evidence_preference: Stories, analogies, before/after comparisons, simple data visualisations

resonates: Relatable examples from everyday life, clear 'so what' for each point, empathy for the reader's starting point
alienates: Assumed knowledge, condescension, information overload, abstract theory without practical application

name: Academic & Research
description: Scholars, researchers, and graduate students who value rigour, proper attribution, and methodological transparency.

characteristics.knowledge_level: High domain expertise — familiar with the literature, methods, and debates in their field
characteristics.expectations: Proper citations, methodological detail, acknowledgement of limitations, contribution to the field
characteristics.priorities: Is this rigorous? Is the evidence sufficient? How does this advance the conversation?

communication.formality: 8/10 — formal academic register, precise language, hedged claims
communication.detail_level: Very high — methodology, data, analysis, limitations all required
communication.evidence_preference: Peer-reviewed sources, statistical analysis, systematic methodology, replicable results

resonates: Thorough literature engagement, transparent methodology, honest about limitations, contribution clearly stated
alienates: Unsupported claims, missing citations, oversimplification of nuanced debates, prescriptive conclusions without evidence

name: Creative Professionals
description: Designers, writers, artists, and creative directors who value craft, inspiration, and aesthetic sensibility.

characteristics.knowledge_level: High creative literacy — understand design thinking, creative process, and aesthetic principles
characteristics.expectations: Visual examples, process insight, creative rationale, inspiration alongside information
characteristics.priorities: Is this beautiful? Is the thinking original? Does it respect the craft?

communication.formality: 4/10 — professional but expressive, room for personality
communication.jargon_tolerance: 6/10 — design and creative terms welcome, technical terms need translation
communication.detail_level: Moderate — enough to understand the thinking, not a specification document
communication.evidence_preference: Visual examples, case studies, process breakdowns, before/after comparisons

resonates: Original thinking, beautiful presentation, respect for the creative process, 'show me, don't just tell me'
alienates: Purely data-driven arguments, ignoring aesthetics, treating design as decoration, template thinking

name: Students & Learners
description: Students, career changers, and self-taught learners who value clear explanations, progressive disclosure, and supportive tone.

characteristics.knowledge_level: Building knowledge — assume basics may need reinforcement, don't assume prior exposure
characteristics.expectations: Step-by-step explanations, examples that build progressively, practice opportunities, clear success criteria
characteristics.priorities: Can I follow this? Will this help me learn? Am I making progress?

communication.formality: 3/10 — warm, encouraging, peer-to-peer rather than teacher-to-student
communication.jargon_tolerance: 2/10 — introduce terms gradually, always define on first use
communication.detail_level: High but structured — break complex topics into digestible chunks
communication.evidence_preference: Worked examples, analogies to familiar concepts, practice exercises, 'try this yourself' prompts

resonates: Clear learning path, celebration of progress, mistakes normalised, 'here is how I learned this'
alienates: Assumed knowledge, gatekeeping, dismissive tone, 'this is obvious' or 'simply do X'
